input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup and reacts to changes automatically — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` extension method or `AppThemeBinding` class:

```csharp
// Option 1: SetAppTheme (simplest)
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 24 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);

// Option 2: SetBinding with AppThemeBinding
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetBinding(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    new AppThemeBinding
    {
        Light = Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
        Dark = Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
    });
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Trigger | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (system setting) |
| Live updates | Yes — switches instantly when user toggles device theme |
| Resource dictionaries | You can also theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside `Style` setters or reference themed `Color` resources |

For larger apps, define colors in `Resources/Styles/Colors.xaml` and reference them, keeping your pages cleaner:

```xml
<!-- Colors.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">
    <!-- won't work directly; use merged approach below -->
</Color>

<!-- Better: use AppThemeBinding in Styles.xaml -->
<Style TargetType="ContentPage">
    <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
</Style>
```

This keeps theme logic centralized and your pages free of repeated bindings.
